services.service_name: Issuance of Building Permits Including Ancillary Permits
services.description: A document issued to a person, firm, or corporation prior to the construction of their building. Covers: (1) Single dwelling residential of not more than 3 storeys, (2) Commercial buildings of not more than 2 storeys, (3) Renovation within a building, and (4) Warehouse not more than 2 storeys high storing non-hazardous substances. Includes inspection and review to comply with approved standard requirements on zoning and land use, lines and grades, structural design, sanitary and sewerage, environmental health, electrical and mechanical safety, and other rules under the National Building Code of the Philippines. Applicable for projects with total floor area not more than 1,500 sq.m.
services.who_may_avail: Any person, firm or corporation including any agency of the government who is planning to erect, construct, alter, repair, move, or demolish any building or structure

requirements.document: Filled-up and Notarized Unified Application Form

Zoning and Locational Clearance and all its supporting documents: (1) Community Tax Certificate, (2) Certificate of Ownership/Tax Declaration/Deed of Sale/Land Title/Lease Contract, (3) Tax Clearance, (4) Barangay Clearance, (5) Design Plans and Specifications including 4 photocopies of valid licenses of all involved professionals, (6) Cost Estimate
BFP Clearance (to be secured during the process)
Mayor's Permit (to be secured during the process)

process_flow.client_action: Submit the filled-up Unified Application Forms and the Zoning and Locational Clearances with all supporting documents.

agency_actions.action: Receive the application forms and supporting documents. Advise the applicant to return after receiving a notification/text message (usually after 1 to 2 days) regarding the result of technical evaluation and assessment of fees.
Evaluate the Building Plan and Specifications.
If it passes the evaluation, proceed with the site inspection.
Prepare the Order of Payment.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the office after receiving the notification/text message.

agency_actions.action: Notify the applicant via text message regarding the evaluation result. If it passes, issue the Order of Payment.

process_flow.client_action: Pay the fees to the Municipal Treasurer's Office.

agency_actions.action: Issue the Order of Payment.
agency_actions.fees: Building Permit Fees – as per the Schedule of Fees and Charges of the National Building Code of the Philippines

process_flow.client_action: Secure BFP Clearance.

agency_actions.action: Advise the applicant to secure BFP Clearance.
agency_actions.fees: Fire Safety Inspection Fee – as per BFP's assessment

process_flow.client_action: Secure Mayor's Permit.

agency_actions.action: Advise the applicant to secure Mayor's Permit.
agency_actions.fees: Mayor's Permit Fee for Construction – as per lot size: 50 sq.m. and below = PHP 120.00; 51 sq.m. to 150 sq.m. = PHP 180.00; above 150 sq.m. = PHP 240.00
agency_actions.person_responsible: Mayor's Office Staff, Office of the Mayor

process_flow.client_action: Submit the BFP Clearance, Mayor's Permit, and all corresponding Official Receipts.

agency_actions.action: If evaluation fails, explain to the applicant, provide written explanation, and advise to have the plan rectified before re-submitting.
Receive the BFP Clearance, Mayor's Permit, and all corresponding Official Receipts, then record and process.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the Permits.

agency_actions.action: Sign and issue the Permits.

services.total_fees: Building Permit Fees (per National Building Code), Fire Safety Inspection Fee (per BFP), Mayor's Permit Fee (PHP 120.00 / PHP 180.00 / PHP 240.00 depending on lot size)
services.legal_basis: National Building Code of the Philippines

faqs.question: What is a Building Permit?
faqs.answer: A Building Permit is a document issued before the construction, alteration, repair, or demolition of a building or structure. It ensures compliance with zoning, structural, sanitary, electrical, and mechanical safety standards under the National Building Code of the Philippines.

faqs.question: Who needs to secure a Building Permit?
faqs.answer: Any person, firm, corporation, or government agency planning to erect, construct, alter, repair, move, or demolish any building or structure must secure a Building Permit.

faqs.question: What types of buildings are covered by this service?
faqs.answer: This service covers single dwelling residential buildings of not more than 3 storeys, commercial buildings of not more than 2 storeys, renovations within a building, and warehouses of not more than 2 storeys storing non-hazardous substances — all with a total floor area not exceeding 1,500 sq.m.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get a Building Permit?
faqs.answer: The process is classified as Complex and takes approximately 5 days, including plan evaluation, site inspection, BFP clearance, and Mayor's permit processing.

faqs.question: How much is the Building Permit fee?
faqs.answer: Building Permit Fees are based on the Schedule of Fees and Charges of the National Building Code of the Philippines. Additionally, a Fire Safety Inspection Fee is assessed by the BFP, and a Mayor's Permit Fee of PHP 120.00 (50 sq.m. and below), PHP 180.00 (51 to 150 sq.m.), or PHP 240.00 (above 150 sq.m.) applies.

faqs.question: What happens if my building plan fails the evaluation?
faqs.answer: The Municipal Engineer will explain the reasons in writing and advise you to have the plan rectified before re-submitting for evaluation.

faqs.question: Do I need to secure a BFP Clearance and Mayor's Permit separately?
faqs.answer: Yes. Both the BFP Clearance and Mayor's Permit are required and must be secured separately during the process. The MEO will advise you accordingly.

services.description: A document issued to a person, firm, or corporation prior to the occupancy of their building. Includes an inspection and review to comply with approved standard requirements under the National Building Code of the Philippines.
services.who_may_avail: Any person, firm or corporation including any agency of the government who wants to occupy or use a newly constructed or old building or structure

requirements.document: Unified Application Form
Certificate of Completion, duly notarized (forms available at this office)
As-Built Plans, duly signed and sealed by respective Engineers
Construction Logbook
Photocopy of valid licenses of all involved professionals
Photograph of the completed structure

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Engineer's Office and inquire about the Occupancy Permit/Certificate of Occupancy.

agency_actions.action: Provide checklist of requirements.

process_flow.client_action: If all requirements are available, apply for the Certificate and submit all requirements.

agency_actions.action: Check the requirements, assess fees, and issue Order of Payment.

process_flow.client_action: Pay to the cashier at the Municipal Treasurer's Office.

agency_actions.action: Receive payment and issue Official Receipt.
agency_actions.fees: As per the National Building Code of the Philippines

process_flow.client_action: Return to the office and submit the Official Receipt.

agency_actions.action: Receive the Official Receipt, fill up the form, forward to the Municipal Engineer, and advise the applicant to wait for issuance.

process_flow.client_action: Secure Fire Safety Inspection Certificate from BFP.

agency_actions.action: Process Fire Safety Inspection Certificate.

process_flow.client_action: Submit the BFP Clearance.

agency_actions.action: Receive the BFP Clearance and all corresponding Official Receipts, then record and process.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the Certificate.

agency_actions.action: Issue the Certificate of Occupancy.

services.total_fees: As per the National Building Code of the Philippines, plus Fire Safety Inspection Fee (per BFP's assessment)
services.legal_basis: National Building Code of the Philippines

faqs.question: What is a Certificate of Occupancy?
faqs.answer: It is a document issued before a building is occupied or used, certifying that the construction complied with approved standards under the National Building Code of the Philippines.

faqs.question: Who needs to get a Certificate of Occupancy?
faqs.answer: Any person, firm, corporation, or government agency that wants to occupy or use a newly constructed or old building or structure must secure this certificate.

faqs.question: Do I need a Fire Safety Inspection Certificate?
faqs.answer: Yes. A Fire Safety Inspection Certificate from the BFP is required and must be submitted before the Certificate of Occupancy can be issued.

faqs.question: What is an As-Built Plan?
faqs.answer: An As-Built Plan is the final engineering plan showing the actual construction as it was built, signed and sealed by the involved licensed engineers.

faqs.question: How long does the process take?
faqs.answer: The process ranges from 2 to 5 days depending on the complexity of the project.

services.description: A document issued to an applicant prior to the installation of electrical wiring and fixtures, including an electric meter, in a building or structure.
services.who_may_avail: Any person, firm or corporation including any agency of the government who is planning to install electrical wiring and fixtures including an electric meter in a building or structure

requirements.document: Certificate of Occupancy, photocopy
Fire Safety Inspection Certificate (to be acquired during the process)

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Engineering Office, apply for this Permit, and submit the requirements.

agency_actions.action: Check requirements.

process_flow.client_action: Pay at the Municipal Treasurer's Office.

agency_actions.action: Issue Order of Payment.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the office and submit the Official Receipt.

agency_actions.action: Receive Official Receipt and fill up the permit form.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the form and secure the required signatures from the following: (2.1) Registered Electrician, (2.2) BFP Fire Safety Inspection Certificate, (2.3) Municipal Engineer, (2.4) Municipal Mayor.

agency_actions.action: Provide the Electrical Wiring Permit form and advise the applicant to secure the signatures of needed personnel.
Registered Electrician's signature (applicant's choice among the pool of private practitioners in the municipality).
agency_actions.fees: As per the Registered Electrician's assessment

agency_actions.action: Fire Safety Inspection Certificate from BFP.
Municipal Engineer's signature.
Municipal Mayor's signature.

process_flow.client_action: Return the signed form to the office and receive the permit.

agency_actions.action: Retain a copy of the permit for filing, then release a copy to the applicant.

services.total_fees: Wiring Fee – PHP 200.00, plus Fire Safety Inspection Fee (per BFP's assessment) and Registered Electrician's fee (per their assessment)

faqs.question: What is an Electrical Wiring Permit?
faqs.answer: It is a permit required before installing electrical wiring, fixtures, or an electric meter in a building or structure.

faqs.question: How much is the Electrical Wiring Permit fee?
faqs.answer: The wiring fee is PHP 200.00, payable at the Municipal Treasurer's Office. Additional fees for the Fire Safety Inspection and the Registered Electrician's service apply separately.

faqs.question: Do I need a Certificate of Occupancy to apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, a photocopy of the Certificate of Occupancy is a requirement for the Electrical Wiring Permit.

faqs.question: Who signs the Electrical Wiring Permit form?
faqs.answer: The form requires signatures from a Registered Electrician (of the applicant's choice), the BFP (Fire Safety Inspection Certificate), the Municipal Engineer, and the Municipal Mayor.

faqs.question: How long does the process take?
faqs.answer: The process takes 3 to 5 days, depending on the availability of the required signatories and the BFP inspection schedule.

services.service_name: Issuance of Certificate of Electrical Inspection (CEI)
services.description: A document issued to an individual, firm, corporation, or government agency who wants to avail of electric service from an electric service provider.
services.who_may_avail: Any person, firm or corporation including any agency of the government who wants to avail electric service from an electric service provider

requirements.document: Electrical Wiring Permit including the Fire Safety Inspection Certificate, photocopy
Approved Electrical Plan/Layout, photocopy

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Engineer's Office, apply for this Certificate, and submit the requirements.

agency_actions.action: Check the requirements and advise the client to return upon receiving a notification/text message, usually within 1 day.
Perform site inspection.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the office to get the result of the inspection.

agency_actions.action: Notify the client regarding the result of the site inspection via text message.

process_flow.client_action: If inspection fails: comply with the required on-site requirements and request for re-inspection.

agency_actions.action: If it fails the inspection, explain and advise the client to comply with the on-site requirements and subject to re-inspection.

process_flow.client_action: If inspection passes: return to the office and pay the fees to the Municipal Treasurer's Office.

agency_actions.action: Issue the Order of Payment.

process_flow.client_action: Submit the Official Receipt to the office.

agency_actions.action: Receive Official Receipt and prepare the form, then give the form to the applicant to secure the signature of a Registered Electrician.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the form and have it signed by a Registered Electrician (applicant's choice among the pool of private practitioners in the municipality).

agency_actions.action: Registered Electrician reviews and signs the form.
agency_actions.fees: As per the Registered Electrician's assessment

process_flow.client_action: Return the signed form to the office and receive the Certificate.

agency_actions.action: Issue the Certificate of Electrical Inspection.

services.total_fees: PHP 200.00 plus Registered Electrician's fee (per their assessment)

faqs.question: What is a Certificate of Electrical Inspection (CEI)?
faqs.answer: It is a certificate required before availing electric service from an electric service provider. It confirms that the electrical installation has passed inspection.

faqs.question: What are the requirements for a CEI?
faqs.answer: You need a photocopy of the Electrical Wiring Permit (including the Fire Safety Inspection Certificate) and a photocopy of the Approved Electrical Plan/Layout.

faqs.question: How much is the fee?
faqs.answer: The fee is PHP 200.00, payable at the Municipal Treasurer's Office after passing the site inspection. An additional fee from the Registered Electrician also applies.

faqs.question: What happens if my electrical installation fails the inspection?
faqs.answer: The MEO Staff will explain the deficiencies and advise you to comply with the on-site requirements before requesting a re-inspection.

faqs.question: How long does the process take?
faqs.answer: The process takes 3 to 6 days, depending on the inspection result and the Registered Electrician's schedule.

services.description: An engineering document containing various information about an infrastructure project, such as the title of the project, cost, technical description, scope of works, quantity take-off, and other data required for the proposed project.
services.who_may_avail: Any government official, employee, or agency planning to implement a government infrastructure project

requirements.document: Kind or type of project
Location of the proposed project
Funding amount and source/s

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Engineer's Office, request for a Program of Works, and submit the needed data.

agency_actions.action: Get the data and discuss the project with the client. Advise the client to return upon receiving a notification/text message.
Conduct site investigation.
agency_actions.person_responsible: MEO Staff / Municipal Engineer, Municipal Engineering Office

agency_actions.action: Prepare draft sketch plan or measurements needed for quantity estimate.
Prepare final Engineering Design/Plan (optional).
agency_actions.processing_time: 1 to 5 days (depending on the scope of the project)

agency_actions.action: Prepare the Quantity Take-Off of every item of works.
Canvas prices of materials.
Finalize the Program of Works.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the office upon receiving the notification.

agency_actions.action: Record and release the Program of Works.

services.total_processing_time: Simple to Complex – varies depending on scope (approximately 5 to 10 days)

faqs.question: What is a Program of Works (POW)?
faqs.answer: A Program of Works is an engineering document that details information about a government infrastructure project, including the project title, cost, scope of works, quantity take-off, and other technical data.

faqs.question: Who can request a Program of Works?
faqs.answer: Any government official, employee, or agency planning to implement a government infrastructure project can request this service.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for the Program of Works?
faqs.answer: No, this service is provided free of charge.

faqs.question: How long does it take to prepare a Program of Works?
faqs.answer: The preparation time varies depending on the scope and complexity of the project. It can take from a few days to about 10 days, including site investigation, design preparation, quantity take-off, material canvassing, and finalization.

faqs.question: What information do I need to provide?
faqs.answer: You need to provide the type of project, the location of the proposed project, and the funding amount and source.

services.service_name: Issuance of Certificate of Project Completion
services.description: A document issued confirming or attesting the completion of a government infrastructure project.
services.who_may_avail: Any government official, employee, or agency that has completed a government infrastructure project

requirements.document: Labor Payroll Voucher
Program of Works, photocopy
Pictures of the project – before it started, during implementation, and after completion

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the office and request for a Certificate of Project Completion.

agency_actions.action: Check the requirements and prepare the Certificate.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the Certificate.

agency_actions.action: Sign and issue the Certificate.

faqs.question: What is a Certificate of Project Completion?
faqs.answer: It is an official document that confirms and attests that a government infrastructure project has been completed.

faqs.question: Who can request a Certificate of Project Completion?
faqs.answer: Any government official, employee, or agency that has completed a government infrastructure project may request this certificate.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to submit?
faqs.answer: You need to submit a Labor Payroll Voucher, a photocopy of the Program of Works, and a set of pictures showing the project before it started, during implementation, and after completion.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for this service?
faqs.answer: No, this service is free of charge.

faqs.question: How long does the process take?
faqs.answer: The Certificate is processed and issued in approximately 15 minutes.
